title: Nucleofection of iPSC
author: Celeste M M. Karch
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.x62frge

steps.title: Cells should be passaged as single cells prior to nucleofection
steps.instruction: Cells should be passaged as single cells prior to nucleofection. Split cells into 3 wells of a 6 well plate 48 hours prior to nucleofection (plan for cells to be confluent in 48 hours). You will need 3 million cells per nucleofection, so 3-6 confluent wells should be sufficient Split 1 well from a 6 well plate into 3 wells in a 6 well plate.

steps.title: Coat 6 well plate with 1...
steps.instruction: Coat 6 well plate with 1 mL Matrigel (supplemented with RGD fragment) for 01:00:00

steps.title: Aspirate media from cell culture
steps.instruction: Aspirate media from cell culture.

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL - 2...
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL - 2 mL of PBS, per well.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:05:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 to achieve single cells. Individual donor cell lines exhibit variable sensitivity to accutase-mediated dissociation. Thus, monitor cells closely to determine when single cell dissociation is achieved.

steps.title: Collect cells in a 5 mL...
steps.instruction: Collect cells in a 5 mL DMEM/F12 and transfer to a 15 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Spin at 750-800 rpm for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Spin at 750-800 rpm for 00:03:00 .

steps.title: Aspirate media
steps.instruction: Aspirate media.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in mTesR1 ( 2...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in mTesR1 ( 2 mL per well) supplemented with 1 uM Rock Inhibitor.

steps.title: Plate 2 mL of cells per...
steps.instruction: Plate 2 mL of cells per well on a Matrigel (supplemented with RGD fragment) coated plate.

steps.title: Change media daily with mTesR1
steps.instruction: Change media daily with mTesR1

steps.title: Coat plate for nucleofection (2-3 wells)...
steps.instruction: Coat plate for nucleofection (2-3 wells) with matrigel supplemented with RGD for 01:00:00

steps.title: Just prior to splitting cells for...
steps.instruction: Just prior to splitting cells for nucleofection, equilibrate Matrigel/RGD coated plate with 3-5 mL/well DMEM/F12+10% FBS supplemented with 10 uM Rock Inhibitor.

steps.title: Aliquot DNA into 1
steps.instruction: Aliquot DNA into 1.7 mL tubes using the table below. %s %s

steps.title: Aspirate media
steps.instruction: Aspirate media.

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL - 2 mL PBS per well
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL - 2 mL PBS per well.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of accutase per well
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of accutase per well.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:05:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 minutes (checking at 5 minutes) to achieve single cells. Individual donor cell lines exhibit variable sensitivity to accutase-mediated dissociation. Thus, monitor cells closely to determine when single cell dissociation is achieved.

steps.title: Collect cells in 5 mL PBS...
steps.instruction: Collect cells in 5 mL PBS and transfer to a 15 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Spin at 750-800 rpm for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Spin at 750-800 rpm for 00:03:00 .

steps.title: Resuspend cell pellet in 3 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellet in 3 mL PBS in 15 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Pipette 10 µL into cell counter
steps.instruction: Pipette 10 µL into cell counter.

steps.title: Using all four corners of the...
steps.instruction: Using all four corners of the countess slide, calculate the average number of cells.

steps.title: Multiply the average by 10,000 (10 4 )
steps.instruction: Multiply the average by 10,000 (10 4 ).

steps.title: Multiply product from step 24 by...
steps.instruction: Multiply product from step 24 by 3 to get the total number of cells. _________ average number of cells x 10,000 x 3 = total number of cells.

steps.title: Take total number of cells calculated...
steps.instruction: Take total number of cells calculated in step 25 and divide by 3 million.

steps.title: Take answer from previous step and...
steps.instruction: Take answer from previous step and divide by 3 to get the volume of cells necessary for nucleofection.

steps.title: Transfer the desired volume of cells to microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the desired volume of cells to microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells at 90 xg for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells at 90 xg for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Aspirate PBS
steps.instruction: Aspirate PBS.

steps.title: Make reaction mix from Lonza Kit
steps.instruction: Make reaction mix from Lonza Kit: P3 Primary Cell 4D (V4XP-3024)- total of 100 µL per nucleofection. If performing multiple nucleofections, make a master mix.

steps.title: 82 µL P3 solution
steps.instruction: 82 µL P3 solution

steps.title: 18 µL of Supplement
steps.instruction: 18 µL of Supplement

steps.title: Combine Reaction Mix from step 31...
steps.instruction: Combine Reaction Mix from step 31 ( 100 µL ) with previously aliquoted DNA from go to step #14

steps.title: Mix Reaction Mix and DNA with...
steps.instruction: Mix Reaction Mix and DNA with cell pellet by pipetting up and down with p200 pipette. Try to pipette as little as possible. Pipette only until mixed.

steps.title: Transfer 100 µL to a cuvette
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 µL to a cuvette.

steps.title: Nucleofect with Lonza program CA-137 in P3 solution
steps.instruction: Nucleofect with Lonza program CA-137 in P3 solution.

steps.title: Add a small amount of media...
steps.instruction: Add a small amount of media to cuvette via dropper in order to obtain all the cells form the cuvette.

steps.title: Transfer cells/DNA solution to appropriate pre-coated...
steps.instruction: Transfer cells/DNA solution to appropriate pre-coated well containing 2 mL of DMEM/F12+10% FBS+10 uM Rock Inhibitor.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C overnight.

steps.title: Continue cultruing the iPSC in 1...
steps.instruction: Continue cultruing the iPSC in 1 well of a 6 well plate for 5-7 days post nucleofection, changing mTesR1 daily. a. 24:00:00 post nucleofection--add mTesR1 with 5 uM Rock Inhibitor. b. 48:00:00 post nucleofection-- add mTesR1 with 2.5 uM Rock Inhibitor. 24 hours after nucleofection, cells exhibit extended processes - this is expected. Over the following 24-72 hours, cells recover and return to typical rounded iPSC morphology. The exact timeline for the morphological recovery is dependent on donor lines. c. 72:00:00 post nucleofection-- add mTesR1 with 1uM Rock Inhibitor.

steps.title: Continue cultruing in mTesR1 until confluent
steps.instruction: Continue cultruing in mTesR1 until confluent. Cells must be maintained for 5 days post-nucleofection prior to screening to minimize chimeric clones.
